questions.q: An IT department is implementing a change management process. They need to classify changes based on impact and urgency (e.g., a routine printer driver update vs. a major network infrastructure upgrade). Which of the following would BEST help categorize these items?
questions.explain: Identifying the change type categorizes changes by complexity, risk, and urgency — routine, standard, or emergency.

questions.q: A small doctor's office wants the highest level of wireless security AND desktop authentication. They only use cloud-based SaaS apps. Which combination should you implement?
questions.explain: WPA2 uses AES with CCMP encryption. Multifactor requires 2+ factors (know / have / are / do). WEP is broken, WPS is brute-forceable.

questions.q: David needs to see diagnostic information about an Android app and its network connections while debugging it on a tablet. Which of the following should he enable on the device?
questions.explain: Developer mode exposes diagnostic info for apps and network connections. Rooting grants admin rights but isn't needed just for diagnostics.

questions.q: Which command is used in the Linux terminal to change the permissions of a file?
questions.explain: chmod = change mode (permissions). chown changes owner. chgrp changes group. chattr changes attributes.

questions.q: Abagail's Microsoft Word crashes every time she opens one specific document. Other documents open fine, no recent changes. She needs the content. What's the BEST next step?
questions.correct: Attempt to open the document in Safe Mode
distractors: Replace the document with a new blank file
questions.explain: Safe Mode bypasses add-ins and corrupt settings — often the quickest path to recovering the document's content.

questions.q: Daily Linux server backups are saved to a local NAS. You need to ensure backups will actually work when needed for restoration. What should you recommend?
questions.correct: Attempt to restore to a test server from one of the backup files
distractors: Set up scripts to automatically reattempt failed backup jobs
Verify the file sizes of each backup match the source
Increase the frequency of backups to twice daily
questions.explain: The ONLY way to be sure a backup is good is to actually restore from it. Size checks and retries don't prove recoverability.

questions.q: Which section of the Control Panel should a technician use to edit the account profile related to a user's Microsoft Outlook client?
questions.explain: The Mail applet is specifically for viewing, editing, and removing Outlook profile settings for the logged-in user.

questions.q: A user logs into Windows 11 and sees a black screen with just a movable cursor — no icons, no taskbar. What's the BEST initial fix to try?
questions.correct: Restart the Windows Explorer process using Task Manager
distractors: Boot into Safe Mode and perform a system restore
Check the monitor cables and display settings
questions.explain: A black screen + cursor usually means explorer.exe didn't start. Restarting it is the least invasive fix and works most of the time.

questions.q: The server room humidity is at 10%. Which of the following risks to the servers could occur due to this low humidity level?
questions.explain: Low humidity allows static buildup → discharge damages components. Corrosion is a HIGH-humidity problem. Target: 40-60% RH.

questions.q: What type of structure is 'IF THEN ELSE' in scripting?
questions.explain: A branch controls program flow based on a logic condition — classically IF / THEN / ELSE. Variables hold values; loops repeat; functions encapsulate.

questions.q: Dion Training wants governance for password length, complexity, and expiration. Which policy should this be documented within?
questions.explain: Password policies define complexity, length, expiration, and protection rules for knowledge-based authentication. AUPs govern use of company resources.

questions.q: An attacker is using a precomputed table of hash values to attempt to crack your Windows password. What type of password attack is this?
questions.explain: Rainbow tables precompute hashes for fast lookup. Brute-force tries combinations live. Dictionary uses word lists. Phishing tricks users.

questions.q: A Windows 10 workstation won't boot. The technician needs to replace the OS files with a new copy of the SAME version, without touching user data or installed apps. Which type of installation should they use?
questions.explain: A repair install replaces system files of the same version while preserving data, settings, and apps. An in-place upgrade installs a NEW version.

questions.q: You run 'ipconfig /all' and see your IP is 169.254.34.12. Why can't you reach the internet?
questions.correct: The workstation couldn't reach the DHCP server
distractors: The workstation couldn't reach the DNS server
questions.explain: 169.254.x.x is an APIPA address — assigned automatically when no DHCP server responds. DNS issues wouldn't give you an APIPA address.

questions.q: Which security control would be the BEST way to prevent unauthorized users from booting a workstation from an external USB drive?
questions.explain: A BIOS password prevents changing the boot order. Encryption protects data at rest but doesn't stop the boot itself. OS passwords are bypassed entirely if you boot from USB.

questions.q: Your team is finalizing a change request to install 20 new APs for a college campus. Locations are documented. What's the MOST important thing to add to the scope of work before approval?
questions.explain: Every change needs a rollback plan so it can be reversed if it goes sideways. Risk analysis is important but the rollback is the critical safety net.

questions.q: Edward can access his company email but cannot reach the network file share that hosted his shared inbox folders. He can reach other network shares. What's the MOST likely cause?
questions.correct: The network file share's permission has been modified
distractors: The internet security options in his email client have been modified
The domain controller's DNS settings were changed
The OS update installed an incompatible email driver
questions.explain: The file share's permissions have likely changed during Edward's vacation. Since he can still send/receive email, his account permissions are fine. Internet security options would affect sending/receiving, and OS updates wouldn't affect network file shares.

questions.q: A technician needs to make a Windows computer visible to other PCs on a workgroup network. Which setting should be enabled?
questions.explain: Network discovery allows Windows to find other computers on a network. File sharing is separate — it's about making your files accessible, not finding other PCs.

questions.q: A file server must remain running during a power outage until the backup generator starts. Which device provides the needed buffer?
questions.explain: A UPS provides battery backup during power outages (until the generator kicks in). A line conditioner only adjusts voltage levels but can't keep the server running during a complete power failure.

questions.q: A Linux administrator needs to change a user's password from the command line. Which command should they use?
questions.explain: passwd changes a user's password. pwd displays the present working directory.

questions.q: An FTP server currently sits on the internal network. To improve security, where should it be placed?
questions.correct: Migrate the FTP server from the internal network to a screened subnet
distractors: Add a deny rule to the firewall's ACL that blocks port 21 outbound
Place the FTP server in the same VLAN as the web server
Restrict FTP access to only the IT department
questions.explain: A screened subnet (DMZ) isolates public-facing servers from the internal network. Blocking outbound port 21 wouldn't prevent exploitation of the FTP server itself.

questions.q: Which file type can be executed on Linux, macOS, and Windows without modification?
questions.explain: Python (.py) scripts run on Linux, macOS, and Windows. Shell scripts (.sh) are Linux/macOS only.

questions.q: A company uses Cisco infrastructure for AAA authentication. Which protocol was developed by Cisco for this purpose?
questions.explain: TACACS+ was developed by Cisco as a proprietary protocol for AAA. RADIUS is a general protocol.

questions.q: What is the most likely way a user's Android tablet could be compromised by a malicious actor?
questions.correct: Use social engineering to trick a user into opening a malicious APK
distractors: Use a tool like ICSSPLOIT to target specific vulnerabilities
Exploit a known kernel vulnerability remotely
Install a malicious app from the Google Play Store
questions.explain: Social engineering (malicious APK) is the most likely attack vector for Android devices.

questions.q: How should a system administrator schedule a vulnerability scan to run automatically every Sunday at 2:00 AM?
questions.explain: Task scheduler runs tasks automatically at specified times. Event viewer logs events.

questions.q: A company wants to deploy Windows and Office settings across 500 devices in Active Directory. Which tool should they use?
questions.explain: Group Policy Objects (GPO) deploy settings across many devices in Active Directory.

questions.q: Which Linux command installs software on an RPM-based distribution?
questions.explain: yum installs software on RPM-based Linux. nano is a text editor.

questions.q: A Linux administrator needs to edit a configuration file from the command line. Which command launches a text editor?
questions.explain: nano is a command-line text editor. cat creates/displays files but doesn't edit them.

questions.q: A chat application has too many users in one conversation, making important messages hard to find. What's the BEST way to organize messages?
questions.correct: Use dedicated channels to keep messages organized.
distractors: Reduce the number of users in each conversation.
Enable read receipts for all users.
Increase the font size in the chat window.
questions.explain: Dedicated channels organize messages so important updates aren't lost.

questions.q: A user reports sudden internet connectivity loss and a warning about high network traffic. What's the BEST first step?
questions.correct: Investigate for malware and run a full security scan on the device
distractors: Turn on airplane mode for a few seconds, then turn it back off
Reset the router to factory settings
questions.explain: Sudden connectivity loss + high traffic warning = possible malware.

questions.q: Which Windows account type can install and remove programs, modify system files, and change permissions?
questions.explain: An Administrator can install/remove programs, modify system files, and change permissions.

questions.q: A user's Google homepage is in a foreign language. They haven't changed their browser settings. What should you check first?
questions.correct: Remove any proxy servers configured in their web browser
distractors: Verify the user's date and time zone are correctly listed in Windows
Change the search engine to Bing
questions.explain: A proxy in a foreign country would cause Google to display in that country's language.

questions.q: After registering the Windows Update service's DLL files, what should a technician do next?
distractors: Register the Windows Update service's DLL files
questions.explain: After registering DLL files, reboot the computer for changes to take effect.

questions.q: A Windows installation reports 'Operating System Not Found.' Which bootrec command rebuilds the Boot Configuration Data?
questions.explain: bootrec /rebuildbcd rebuilds the Boot Configuration Data to detect Windows installations.

questions.q: Which technical control BEST prevents virus outbreaks by closing known vulnerabilities?
questions.explain: Patch management is a technical control that prevents known vulnerabilities from being exploited.

questions.q: After a security update, a workstation fails to boot. What should a technician do first?
questions.correct: Reboot the workstation into safe mode and roll back the recent security update
distractors: Reboot the workstation into the BIOS and reconfigure boot options
Perform a clean installation of Windows
questions.explain: Roll back the problematic update via Safe Mode.

questions.q: A company wants physical access control for its server room. Which device is designed for this purpose?
questions.explain: A server lock is a physical locking mechanism for server cabinets.

questions.q: What security measure has the best ROI for reducing the risk of successful attacks against an organization?
questions.explain: End users are the most vulnerable attack vector; training has the best ROI.

questions.q: Which statement about virtual machines and physical servers is TRUE regarding downtime?
questions.correct: A virtual machine has less downtime than a physical server
distractors: A virtual machine has more downtime than a physical server
Virtual machines cannot be restored after failure
questions.explain: VMs can be restored much faster than physical servers.

questions.q: Before granting remote access to a server, what is the MOST important security measure to implement?
questions.correct: Install the latest security updates and patches to the server
distractors: Set the server's anti-virus software to automatically update itself and perform a full scan every Saturday night
Enable two-factor authentication on the admin account
Configure a firewall rule to allow only specific IP addresses
questions.explain: Keeping the server patched is the most important security measure before granting remote access.

questions.q: An AI tool is generating responses with incorrect or outdated information. What is this issue called?
questions.explain: The issue involves incorrect/outdated AI-generated content, not fabrication of false details.

questions.q: What are the BEST security measures to protect data on a smartphone that could be lost or stolen?
questions.correct: Configure the ability to perform a remote wipe, Enable full device encryption
distractors: Require complex passwords, Enable a pattern lock
Install a VPN, Enable two-factor authentication
Enable a firewall, Update the operating system
questions.explain: Full encryption protects data if the device is lost; remote wipe erases data before a thief can access it.

questions.q: A single Android app crashes repeatedly. What's the least disruptive first step?
questions.correct: Clear the application's cache and data
distractors: Restart the smartphone to refresh the operating system
Reinstall the app from the Play Store
questions.explain: Cache corruption is the most common cause of single-app crashes; clearing cache/data is the least disruptive first step.

questions.q: Which two factors MOST commonly compromise wireless networks?
questions.correct: WPS enabled, Router with outdated firmware
distractors: Default administrative login credentials, Router with outdated firmware
questions.explain: WPS PINs are vulnerable to brute force attacks; outdated firmware is another common vulnerability.

questions.q: Which Windows 10 edition supports up to 4 physical CPUs and 6 TB of RAM?
questions.explain: Windows 10 Pro for Workstations supports up to 4 physical CPUs and 6 TB RAM.

questions.q: A company wants to prevent USB-based malware attacks and password-based compromises. Which two measures should they implement?
questions.correct: Lockout the account after 3 failed login attempts, Change the default credentials on the servers
distractors: Modify the AutoRun settings, Install the operating system security updates
Enable a firewall, Use complex passwords
Install antivirus, Enable full disk encryption
questions.explain: AutoRun settings prevent USB attacks; account lockout and changing default credentials prevent password attacks.

questions.q: Which type of data is regulated under privacy laws such as GDPR and PCI-DSS?
questions.explain: PII (Personally Identifiable Information) is regulated data along with PCI, GDPR, and PHI.

questions.q: An attacker uses a list of common passwords to try to crack a user's account. What type of attack is this?
questions.explain: A dictionary attack uses a list of common passwords; brute-force tries all possible combinations.

questions.q: Which type of network hides your computer and prevents file sharing?
questions.explain: A public network hides your computer and prevents file sharing.

questions.q: An instructor wants only their IoT device to connect to a classroom wireless network while blocking students' devices. Which control should they use?
questions.explain: MAC filtering allows specific devices (instructors') while blocking others (students).

questions.q: A Windows system administrator needs to scan for and restore corrupted system files. Which command should they use?
questions.explain: sfc (System File Checker) scans for and restores corrupted Windows system files. chkdsk checks the file system.

questions.q: An individual is setting up a new personal tablet. They want a screen lock method that balances good security with ease of recall. Which type of screen lock should they use?
questions.explain: A PIN code balances security with ease of recall. It is more secure than a swipe pattern and easier to remember than a complex password, while biometrics may be less convenient in some situations.

questions.q: A company's security policy requires all internet traffic to pass through a central monitoring point. Which setting should be configured on employee workstations to comply with this policy?
questions.explain: A proxy server routes workstation internet traffic through a central point for monitoring and control. DNS and DHCP do not provide that traffic-control function, and a VPN is primarily for secure network access.

questions.q: A computer engineer is explaining the advantages of using SRAM in a CPU's cache memory. What characteristic of SRAM makes it particularly suitable for this role?
questions.correct: Its faster access speed compared to DRAM
distractors: Its ability to store data without power
questions.explain: SRAM is used for CPU cache because it can access data faster than DRAM. It is not nonvolatile and typically has lower storage density than DRAM.

questions.q: A small business wants to consolidate various security functions, including firewall, antivirus, and intrusion prevention, into a single appliance. What type of device should they consider implementing?
questions.explain: A UTM, or Unified Threat Management device, combines multiple security functions such as firewall, antivirus, intrusion prevention, and related protections into one appliance.

questions.q: A computer is experiencing issues with network connectivity. To use the built-in diagnostics and resolve the problem, which option should a user select in the Network and Sharing Center?
distractors: Set up a new connection or network
questions.explain: Troubleshoot problems launches Windows Network Diagnostics to detect and attempt to resolve common network connectivity issues.

questions.q: A user's operating system consistently fails to complete updates. What should be checked first to resolve this issue?
questions.correct: Available storage space on the device
distractors: The compatibility of installed applications with the new update
The speed of the internet connection
The accuracy of the system clock
questions.explain: Failed operating system updates are commonly caused by insufficient free storage. Updates often need significant space to download, unpack, and install.

questions.q: A small business owner is planning to upgrade the computers in her office. She requires an operating system that can handle advanced networking features and remote desktop access, but does not need advanced security features like device guard. Which edition of Windows 10 would be most suitable for her needs?
questions.explain: Windows 10 Pro supports small-business features such as domain join, Group Policy, and hosting Remote Desktop without requiring Enterprise-only security features.

questions.q: A technician suspects that a computer is not connected to the network. To confirm network connectivity to the local router, which command should they execute?
questions.explain: Ping sends ICMP echo requests to a target such as the local router. A reply confirms basic network connectivity to that device.

questions.q: A Windows 10 user wants to create a new profile in Microsoft Outlook. Which option within the Control Panel's Mail utility allows them to do this?
questions.explain: Show Profiles in the Control Panel Mail utility lets users add, remove, or switch Outlook profiles.

questions.q: When a user switches to a new mobile phone and wants to retain their existing phone number and service, what component should they transfer to the new device?
questions.explain: The SIM stores subscriber identity and network authorization information, allowing the user to keep the same phone number and mobile service on a compatible new phone.

questions.q: A Windows 10 user wants to ensure their computer's time is always accurate, even after traveling to different time zones. Which setting in Time and Language should they enable?
distractors: Adjust for daylight saving time automatically
questions.explain: Set time zone automatically lets Windows detect the current time zone and adjust the clock when traveling. Set time automatically only synchronizes the clock within the configured time zone.

questions.q: A technician needs to check the processor type and speed, and the amount of installed RAM on a Windows 10 computer. Which tab in the System Information should they consult?
questions.explain: System Summary in System Information provides the main hardware overview, including processor details and installed RAM.

questions.q: A user is setting up a new Windows 10 computer and is asked to create an account. They want an account that can be used to synchronize settings and files across multiple devices. Which type of account should they choose?
questions.explain: A Microsoft account links the user to Microsoft online services and can synchronize settings and files across devices. A local account is limited to one device.

questions.q: An individual plans to sell their Mac and wants to completely erase all the data on the hard drive to prevent personal information from being recovered by the new owner. Which Disk Utility option should they use?
questions.explain: The Erase option in Disk Utility removes the data from the selected drive, which is the appropriate step before selling or disposing of a Mac.

questions.q: A user wants to organize their open applications and documents into separate workspaces to reduce clutter on their Mac. Which feature should they use?
questions.explain: Multiple Desktops, also known as Spaces, lets macOS users organize windows into separate workspaces to reduce clutter.

questions.q: A network administrator wants to allow a specific application to communicate through the Windows Firewall. Which feature should they configure in the firewall settings?
questions.correct: Advanced settings > Inbound Rules > New Rule
distractors: Advanced settings > Outbound Rules > New Rule
questions.explain: To allow an application to receive network traffic through Windows Firewall, create a new inbound rule from Advanced settings.

questions.q: What is the purpose of implementing Network Access Control (NAC) in a corporate network?
questions.correct: To control which devices are allowed to connect to the network
distractors: To increase the storage capacity of the network
To encrypt the data transmitted over the network
questions.explain: Network Access Control manages which devices are authorized and compliant enough to connect to corporate network resources.

questions.q: Which of the following is a limitation of the Master Boot Record (MBR) partitioning scheme compared to the GUID Partition Table (GPT)?
questions.correct: MBR has a maximum disk size of 2TB
distractors: MBR does not support booting from external drives
MBR can only be used on Windows operating systems
MBR supports a maximum of 128 primary partitions
questions.explain: MBR supports a maximum disk size of 2TB and only four primary partitions. GPT supports much larger disks and many more partitions.

questions.q: A technician needs to partition a 4TB hard drive for use as a data storage in a modern Windows system. Which partitioning style is most suitable for this purpose?
questions.explain: GPT is the correct partitioning style for a 4TB drive because it supports disks larger than 2TB. FAT32 and NTFS are file systems, not partitioning styles.

questions.q: When setting up a wireless network, which encryption method is known for being the strongest and is used in WPA2 and WPA3 for securing Wi-Fi networks?
questions.explain: AES is the strongest common Wi-Fi encryption method and is used with WPA2 and WPA3. TKIP is older and weaker, while WEP is obsolete and insecure.

questions.q: A Windows 10 user needs to adjust the transparency effects on their system for better visibility. Which section in Personalization allows them to modify this setting?
questions.explain: Transparency effects are controlled from the Colors section in Windows Personalization settings.

questions.q: What is the primary function of Network Address Translation (NAT) in a router?
questions.correct: To convert private IP addresses to public IP addresses for internet communication
distractors: To control access to the network
To encrypt all traffic leaving the network
To assign MAC addresses to devices
questions.explain: NAT translates private internal IP addresses to a public IP address so local devices can communicate over the internet.

questions.q: An IT administrator implements Single Sign-On (SSO) for the company's various applications. What is the main advantage of SSO in this corporate environment?
questions.correct: It reduces the number of passwords users need to remember
distractors: It increases the complexity of the login process
It requires a separate password for each application
It eliminates the need for account permissions
questions.explain: SSO lets a user authenticate once and then access multiple applications, reducing password fatigue and simplifying the login experience.

questions.q: A user is experiencing issues with their computer's file system. Which command-line tool should they use to check the integrity of the file system and fix logical file system errors?
questions.explain: chkdsk checks the disk and file system for logical errors and can repair them. sfc scans protected system files instead.

questions.q: A law firm is considering implementing an advanced document management system. What should be the primary business consideration in this decision?
questions.correct: The potential for improving document accessibility and security
distractors: The compatibility of the system with existing hardware
The color scheme of the user interface
The popularity of the vendor on social media
questions.explain: For a law firm, the key business issue is improving secure access to sensitive documents while protecting confidentiality.

questions.q: A network administrator wants to write a script that will automatically install a set of applications on all new employee workstations. Which scripting use case does this scenario best represent?
questions.explain: This script is specifically being used to automate application deployment on new workstations.

questions.q: What is the keyboard shortcut to directly force quit an application on a Mac without opening the Force Quit window?
questions.correct: Command + Option + Shift + Esc (hold for 3 seconds)
questions.explain: Holding Command + Option + Shift + Esc for 3 seconds force quits the frontmost Mac application directly. Option + Command + Esc opens the Force Quit window instead.

questions.q: In a company, an IT administrator needs to manage access to corporate resources on a Windows 10 device. Which option in the Accounts settings should they use?
questions.explain: Access work or school is the Accounts setting used to connect and manage organizational accounts and related resource access.

questions.q: A power user wants to set up a RAID array to increase the storage capacity and reliability of their Mac. Which feature in Disk Utility should they use to configure this?
questions.explain: RAID Assistant in Disk Utility is the correct tool for creating and managing RAID arrays on a Mac.

questions.q: A computer is experiencing severe system issues and cannot boot properly. The user wants to restore the system to its factory settings without using external media. Which method should they use?
questions.explain: A recovery partition contains the factory recovery image and allows the system to be restored without external installation media.

questions.q: After multiple unsuccessful attempts to remove a severe malware infection, a technician decides that the best course of action is to start fresh. Which method should they use to completely remove the infection and restore the system to a known good state?
distractors: Quarantine the files and continue normal use
questions.explain: A full OS reinstallation provides the cleanest path back to a known good state when serious malware cannot be reliably removed any other way.

questions.q: Why is it important for a business to ensure all its software licenses are valid and non-expired?
questions.correct: To avoid legal and financial penalties from software audits
distractors: To keep the software up to date with the latest features
questions.explain: Maintaining valid licenses protects the business from legal exposure and fines during software compliance audits.

questions.q: A technician is instructing a user on how to reset their File Explorer view settings to defaults. What is the correct step under the File Explorer Options?
questions.correct: Under the View tab, click Reset Folders
distractors: Under the View tab, click Apply to Folders
Under the General tab, click Restore Defaults
Under the Search tab, click Reset Index
questions.explain: Reset Folders returns File Explorer folder views to their default settings. Apply to Folders copies the current view to similar folders instead.

questions.q: A user wishes to record their gameplay on a Windows 10 PC. Which built-in Windows feature allows them to do this without installing third-party software?
questions.explain: Game Bar is the built-in Windows feature used to capture gameplay, screenshots, and related recording tasks.

questions.q: A company is setting up a system that allows employees to access their work desktop environments remotely. Which technology enables this remote access to desktop interfaces hosted on central servers?
questions.explain: VDI, or Virtual Desktop Infrastructure, hosts desktop environments on central servers so users can remotely access them from other devices.

questions.q: In a large organization with many departments, an IT administrator needs to apply different security policies to different groups of users. What Active Directory structure should they use to organize users and computers for this purpose?
questions.explain: Organizational units let administrators group users and computers so different Group Policy settings can be applied to different departments.

questions.q: An IT professional is tasked with choosing an operating system for a new high-performance computing environment that will be used for scientific simulations. The system requires resilient file system support, expanded hardware support, and faster file sharing capabilities. Which Windows 10 edition should the IT professional choose?
questions.explain: Windows 10 Pro for Workstations is designed for high-end hardware and adds capabilities such as ReFS support and SMB Direct for demanding workloads.

questions.q: A company implements a policy to ensure all remote desktop connections to its computers are monitored and controlled. Which type of rule should be configured in the local OS firewall?
questions.explain: To monitor and control remote desktop connections according to company policy, an inbound rule specific to Remote Desktop should be configured in the local OS firewall.

questions.q: A user wants their Windows 10 laptop to save energy by not using the battery when it's not being actively used, but also wants to resume work quickly. Which Power Option feature should they use?
questions.explain: Sleep or suspend saves power while keeping the current state in RAM, allowing the user to resume work quickly.

questions.q: An IT administrator needs to segment a large office network to improve performance and increase security. What technology allows for creating separate broadcast domains within the same physical network?
questions.explain: VLANs create separate broadcast domains on the same physical network, which improves performance and helps isolate traffic for better security.

questions.q: A company is upgrading its server storage and needs a solution that offers high performance and reliability for mission-critical applications. Which interface type is commonly used in enterprise environments for such requirements?
questions.explain: SAS is commonly used for enterprise storage because it provides strong performance, reliability, and redundancy features for critical workloads.

questions.q: An IT specialist is setting up a remote access connection for a user who needs to connect to the company network from home. Which protocol can be used to secure this remote connection?
questions.explain: PPP is used to establish direct remote access connections and supports authentication, encryption, and compression for secure communication.

questions.q: An IT administrator wants to assign a personal storage space on the network for each user in the company, which is accessible from any computer within the domain. Which Active Directory feature allows them to accomplish this?
questions.explain: A home folder gives each domain user a personal network storage location that follows them to any computer they log into.

questions.q: A software application on a user's computer is not functioning correctly, potentially due to corrupted files. The user wants to fix the program without removing or altering its data. Which feature in Programs and Features should they use?
questions.explain: The Repair option is intended to fix damaged or missing program files without removing the application or its data.

questions.q: An employee needs to access a file stored on a company server named "Server01" in a shared folder called "Projects." Which of the following is the correct network path they should type into File Explorer to access this folder?
questions.explain: Windows UNC paths use the format \\ServerName\ShareName when accessing shared folders over the network.

questions.q: A remote worker is setting up their home office for frequent video conferences. To ensure their voice is clearly heard by others, what equipment should they prioritize?
questions.explain: For clear communication in frequent video conferences, a good microphone is the most important accessory for transmitting the user's voice clearly.

questions.q: What technology does a Liquid Crystal Display (LCD) primarily use to produce images?
distractors: Electron beams striking a phosphorescent screen
Compressed air to create visual effects
questions.explain: LCDs use liquid crystals to modulate a backlight and produce the final image.

questions.q: After upgrading their computer's RAM from 4GB to 8GB, a user notices that the system still only recognizes 4GB of RAM. What are two likely causes of this issue?
questions.correct: The additional RAM is not compatible with the motherboard; The operating system is a 32-bit version
distractors: The additional RAM is not compatible with the motherboard; The new RAM modules are faulty
The hard drive is full; The operating system is a 32-bit version
The new RAM modules are faulty; The power supply unit is inadequate
questions.explain: A 32-bit operating system often cannot use more than about 4GB of RAM, and incompatible RAM may also prevent the full memory upgrade from being recognized.

questions.q: A salesperson is traveling and needs to connect their laptop to the internet, but there is no Wi-Fi available. What are two viable options for them to access the internet?
questions.correct: Tether their smartphone to the laptop; Use a mobile hotspot device
distractors: Connect to a public Wi-Fi network; Use a mobile hotspot device
Use a wired Ethernet connection; Upgrade the laptop's RAM
Connect to a public Wi-Fi network; Tether their smartphone to the laptop
questions.explain: Without Wi-Fi, the practical mobile options are smartphone tethering or a dedicated mobile hotspot that uses cellular data.

questions.q: A company's IT department is planning a strategy for software maintenance on its networked computers. What practice should they prioritize to maintain system security?
questions.correct: Regularly apply software updates and patches
distractors: Limit internet access to reduce the risk of malware
Disconnect computers from the network when not in use
Manually check for software updates annually
questions.explain: Regular patching closes known vulnerabilities and is one of the core practices for maintaining system security.

questions.q: In order to improve the visual quality of games on a Windows 10 system, which setting under the Gaming section allows users to specify the preferred graphics performance for individual games?
questions.explain: Graphics settings lets Windows users choose graphics performance preferences for specific apps and games.

questions.q: A user reports that a frequently used application on their Windows computer is suddenly crashing and displaying error messages. The IT technician suspects an issue with the application's configuration or corrupted files. What should be the technician's first step in troubleshooting this unexpected application behavior?
questions.correct: Check for and apply any updates to the application
distractors: Run the application in compatibility mode
questions.explain: Checking for application updates is the least disruptive first step and often resolves crashes caused by known bugs or broken components.

questions.q: An administrator needs to add, delete, and modify user accounts on a Windows computer. Which command should they use?
questions.explain: The net user command is specifically used to manage local user accounts from the Windows command line.

questions.q: In a scenario where a user wants to change the size of text, apps, and other items on their Windows 10 display for easier viewing, which option in "Personalization" should they access?
questions.explain: Display settings contains scale and layout controls for adjusting the size of text, apps, and other interface elements.

questions.q: A small business is setting up a network for file and printer sharing among Windows computers. Which protocol is commonly used in such networks to facilitate this sharing?
questions.explain: SMB is the standard Windows network protocol used for file and printer sharing across local networks.

questions.q: A company is concerned about the loss of mobile devices issued to its field workers. Besides implementing physical security measures, which feature should be enabled on these devices for additional security?
questions.explain: Locator applications help recover lost mobile devices by providing tracking information, which directly addresses the device-loss concern.

questions.q: A company needs to authenticate and authorize users connecting to its network remotely via VPN. Which authentication protocol is best suited for centralizing the authentication and authorization process for these remote connections?
questions.explain: RADIUS is widely used to centralize authentication and authorization for remote access services such as VPN connections.

questions.q: A user complains that their Windows 10 search is not returning results from documents stored in a newly added secondary drive. Which option in the "Indexing Options" utility should they modify to resolve this issue?
questions.explain: Modify lets the user choose which drives and folders are included in indexing, which is the direct fix for a newly added drive being omitted from search results.

questions.q: In an older network infrastructure, a technician encounters a system that uses a protocol for allowing applications on different computers to communicate within a local area network. This protocol, primarily used in early Windows networks, is:
questions.explain: NetBIOS was used in early Windows networking to let applications communicate and identify systems across a local network.

questions.q: A user is experiencing issues with a network application after enabling the Windows Defender Firewall. The technician suspects that the firewall might be blocking the application. Which feature in the Windows Defender Firewall should the technician use to check if the application is being blocked?
distractors: Allow an app or feature through Windows Defender Firewall
questions.explain: The Monitoring section in Windows Defender Firewall with Advanced Security shows the active rules and connection status needed to see whether traffic is being blocked.

questions.q: What is the most effective physical destruction method for completely destroying a variety of media, including paper documents, CDs, and hard drives, to ensure data cannot be recovered?
questions.explain: Incineration destroys many different media types completely, making it the most universally effective physical destruction method in this scenario.

questions.q: A large corporation is looking to upgrade its computer systems and requires an operating system with comprehensive security features including Windows Information Protection (WIP), Credential Guard, and Device Guard. Which Windows 10 edition should they opt for?
questions.explain: Windows 10 Enterprise includes advanced security features such as WIP, Credential Guard, and Device Guard that large organizations commonly require.
